mapping.a_element: strongly interacting 1D electrons whose propagator is sought
mapping.b_element: strongly interacting light quarks whose Green function is sought

mapping.a_element: exchange coupling J / Fermi energy E_F setting the energy scales
mapping.b_element: strong coupling constant alpha_s / critical value alpha_c=0.43

mapping.a_element: spectral function (Im of the electron Green function)
mapping.b_element: quark Green function and its mass function

brief_justification: The overlap is only object-level (both compute a strongly-interacting fermion Green function and reference a coupling scale), not machinery. Paper A's method is bosonization of the charge sector plus exact spin treatment (spin-charge separation) to extract spectral-function peaks/shadow bands, whereas Paper B numerically solves Gribov's nonlinear integral (Schwinger-Dyson-type) equation and finds a supercritical threshold at alpha_c above which chiral symmetry breaks; there is no role-for-role correspondence between an operator-bosonization computation and a supercritical gap-equation transition.
